title: Crucial Communism Teaching Act
plain_summary: This law tells a special foundation to create school lessons for high school students about communism and other forms of government where leaders have total control. The lessons will include real stories from people who lived under these types of governments. The goal is to help students learn how these governments are different from America's democracy where people have freedom. Students will better understand why American values like freedom and democracy are important.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Crucial Communism Teaching Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Purposes
sections.text: This law has three main goals. First, it wants to help families, community groups, local communities, school districts, high schools, and state education departments prepare high school students to be responsible and informed citizens. Second, it wants to make sure high school students in the United States learn that communism has caused the deaths of more than one hundred million people around the world. Students should understand the dangers of communism and similar political systems. Students should also understand that one billion five hundred million people still live under communist governments. Third, it wants students to understand that these political systems conflict with freedom and democracy.

sections.title: Section 3 Development and Dissemination of Civic Education Curriculum and Oral History Resources
sections.text: The Victims of Communism Memorial Foundation will create educational materials for high schools. This foundation was created by an earlier law called the FRIENDSHIP Act. The foundation must do three things. First, it must create a civics curriculum for high school students. This curriculum must compare different political systems including communism and totalitarianism (governments with complete control over people) with the principles of freedom and democracy that America was founded on. The curriculum must be accurate, relevant, and easy to understand. It must work with different types of classes like social studies, government, history, and economics. Second, the foundation must create oral history resources that go with the curriculum. These resources will include personal stories called Portraits in Patriotism from different people who show civic minded qualities, who have suffered under the political systems mentioned earlier, and who can compare those systems with America's political system. Third, the foundation must work with state and local education leaders to help high schools use both the curriculum and the personal story resources.

sections.title: Section 4 Definitions
sections.text: The word meanings used in section eight thousand one hundred one of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of nineteen sixty five will also apply to this law.
